title: Third Impact without Union of Adam and Lilith
display_title: Third Impact without Union of Adam and Lilith
wikitext:
[]: Third Impact without Union of Adam and Lilith             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Fri Nov 30, 2012 6:32 am What would happen if Rei had simply merged with Lilith without the Adam Embryo? Why exactly was that needed? Couldn't Instrumentality/Third Impact have been intiated without it? Or could an Anti-AT Field not be generated without Adam or something?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Nov 30, 2012 9:06 am Gendo needed Adam to merge with Lilith so that together they could equal the power of the ancestral race. If he could do it with just Rei he would have jumped at the chance.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Nov 30, 2012 9:10 am If Rei reunited with Lilith she'd have the soul and body of Lilith, thus becoming Lilith herself, the mother of humanity and bearer of the fruit of knowledge. To initiate instrumentality and third impact she'd also need Adam's fruit of life, so yeah, they had to merge. edit: I was ninja'd, but there ya have it. To summarize it, a being with both, the fruit of life and the fruit of knowledge is a godlike being, with the power to do all sorts of things, instrumentality included. All Lilith could do by herself was create life, and maybe explode one thing or two.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 30, 2012 10:07 am So if Lilith could initiate Instrumentality on her own, what role did Eva-01 and Shinji play? With Zeruel's Fruit of Life and Shinji's Fruit of Knowledge, Eva-01 was a being equivalent in power to the Lilith-Adam hybrid, right? Even so, it seemed to me like Eva-01 played the part of a "key" of sorts.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 30, 2012 10:34 am bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: Gendo needed Adam to merge with Lilith so that together they could equal the power of the ancestral race.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 30, 2012 10:52 am If Rei had merged with Lilith on her own she'd revert to the state she was in prior to Unit 01's creation and Yui's Contact Experiment. Nothing else would happen. My speculation is that Seele attempted to trigger Instrumentality using Lilith alone, but found it wouldn't work since she would not go that route on a world seeded with her type of life (i.e. she's done her job already, so why would she want to undo it?). Once they unraveled this using the SDSS they no doubt despaired until they discovered Adam, which they could use to get Lilith to do what they wanted (i.e. reset life on the planet so the two Seeds could hash out competing directives). But they had to be tricky about it: if they just woke Adam directly there's no telling how things would go, and of course the Lance would stop them cold since it's there to make sure this sort of thing doesn't happen without supervision. So they needed to awaken Adam, fool the Lance into letting them proceed (hence the Donor; the Lance couldn't (wouldn't) shut down Lilin DNA, and so couldn't stop Adam since the two were intermingled at the time), dispose of Adam/get him into a controlable form, then merge him with Lilith on their terms to make the process proceed as they wished. That was the point of 2I -- awaken Adam, reduce him to an embryonic state, deal with the fallout (Angels), then do 3I the way they wanted to do it all along. If an Angel had contacted Lilith it might have been able to force a reset, but it would have needed Lilith's complete soul to make that happen (which IMO is why Rei mysteriously shows up during Angel ends in the NGE2 game). But with no Angel souls around this would be kind of pointless (though it might have gotten her to reform Lilin souls into Angel-style bodies, who knows). Similarly, if a Lilin made contact with Adam with the latter in a proper body (i.e. not a clone or the embryo, but the real deal complete with soul) he or she might also have been able to force a reset (as we see in 2I), though that would probably go badly for said Lilin since Adam had Angel-style souls on hand and presumably would have used them instead of doing whatever the Lilin wanted. In 3I Gendo delivered the neatly-wrapped embryo/soul package to Rei, and she in turn delivered them to Lilith (along with Lilith's soul). At this point Lilith went "oh hey, reset protocol" and 3I. But there were no Angel souls around and Adam was good and whipped so that left her going "wait, do you really wanna do this?" at which point Shinji. And then she does it and everyone goes TANG and then she's like "okay, last chance, do you really REALLY wanna do this?" and Shinji's like "oh, never mind" and EoEoE. Then Lilith's like "fuck this shit, I'm out" and gives all her power to Yui and dies. And now Yui's just Yui but all seedy and shit and I have no idea what she expects to do with that since she doesn't have any souls to work with, but whatever. Nice job, smarty pants. You really didn't think this through, did you? And that's impact mechanics in a nutshell (for NGE at least; no fucking clue with NTE just yet).

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Nov 30, 2012 11:49 am Bagheera wrote: In 3I Gendo delivered the neatly-wrapped embryo/soul package to Rei

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Fri Nov 30, 2012 11:56 am Possibly a sub-motive of SEELE was to have Kaworu killed so Adam's soul could assimilate with Adam's body in Gendo. Plausible given Kaworu's mole-like status in NERV, with SEELE putting him there to be close enough to Adam-body. I think SEELE knew Eva-01 would kill Tabris, so they developed a contrived plan to rejoin her pieces.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:11 pm Jinroh wrote: How exactly? Kaworu had just died... are we supposed to assume his soul was harvested and put back in the embryo?

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:18 pm That would explain why the giant Lilith can switch to Kaworu. Do we know what Seele knew about the Adam embryo? I don't think they were aware Gendo fused with it... is it known? I don't remember the details.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:29 pm During the scene in Ep.24' where Kaworu converses with the monoliths, one of them mentions, cryptically, that Adam is within Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:30 pm Bagheera wrote: But there were no Angel souls around

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:31 pm Jinroh wrote: That would explain why the giant Lilith can switch to Kaworu. Do we know what Seele knew about the Adam embryo? I don't think they were aware Gendo fused with it... is it known? I don't remember the details.

[]:             Quote Post by gorgeousshutin » Fri Nov 30, 2012 1:38 pm That would explain why the giant Lilith can switch to Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:16 pm At least one ending in NGE2 PSP had Rei merge with Lilith (without Adam being involved)--but Lilith ended up dying/melting due to Rei's influence. (Rei's Good End. Lilith wanted the Angel's Fruit of Life, Rei explained it wasn't needed and how she's her own person, etc, etc.) In Misato's Good End, Rei merges with Lilith (not sure if with Adam, too), becoming GNR. But she just stands there doing nothing while saying something about "her child (this planet?) is alright". Not sure the context other than Seele freaking out.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:44 pm I remember reading in the wiki about the NGE2 game and the CI files, and it mentioned that their canonicity is "tier 2". What exactly is that supposed to mean? That it's canon but not so much? How does it work?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:51 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: At least one ending in NGE2 PSP had Rei merge with Lilith (without Adam being involved)--but Lilith ended up dying/melting due to Rei's influence. (Rei's Good End. Lilith wanted the Angel's Fruit of Life, Rei explained it wasn't needed and how she's her own person, etc, etc.) In Misato's Good End, Rei merges with Lilith (not sure if with Adam, too), becoming GNR. But she just stands there doing nothing while saying something about "her child (this planet?) is alright". Not sure the context other than Seele freaking out.

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Fri Nov 30, 2012 8:15 pm Jinroh wrote: That would explain why the giant Lilith can switch to Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Dec 01, 2012 4:34 pm Yeah, a soul is supposed to be able to shape PWM, as seen when Lilith absorbs Rei. What's unclear is how Adam(?) manifested himself as Kaworu, which would seem to go contrary to the theory. Kaworu/Tabris was supposed to be the vessel for Adam's soul, but assuming Gendo restored Adam's soul to the embryo, then why does Lilith/Adam switch to Kaworu, and not Adam? I'm going to say a meta-answer is the most plausible, as the producers probably decided Kaworu appearing would better suit the setting. Shinji reacts completely differently when he sees the giant Kaworu, and is calmed by his appearance.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 01, 2012 4:47 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Think about it psychologically. (snip)
